title: License activation fails due to DNS resolution issue
symptom: When trying to activate the license in n8n running on Docker, the error 'getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN enterprise.n8n.io' occurs, despite having internet access.
applies_when: Running n8n in a Docker container
root_cause: The Docker container's DNS settings may be isolated or misconfigured, potentially due to interference from local DNS services like AdGuard Home. This prevents n8n from resolving the domain needed for license activation.
fix_steps: Add the following to your Docker Compose file under the service: `extra_hosts: - "enterprise.n8n.io:104.26.13.187" - "enterprise.n8n.io:104.26.12.187" - "enterprise.n8n.io:172.67.68.102"`
Set public DNS in the container by adding `dns: - 1.1.1.1 - 8.8.8.8` to your Docker Compose file.
Optionally, add `NODE_OPTIONS: "--dns-result-order=ipv4first"` to force IPv4 resolution.
Remove or comment out the variable `N8N_HIDE_USAGE_PAGE` if it was set.
agent_instruction: Update your Docker Compose configuration with the specified DNS settings and redeploy the stack.
